steps.title: Apply the pyramid volume formula
steps.content:
The volume of a pyramid is $V = \frac{1}{3} \times \text{base area} \times \text{height}$. For a square base with side length $s$, the base area is $s^2$, so:
$$V = \frac{1}{3}s^2h$$
Substitute the given values $V = 128$ and $h = 6$:
$$128 = \frac{1}{3}s^2(6)$$

steps.title: Simplify and solve for the side length
steps.content:
Simplify the right side:
$$128 = \frac{6s^2}{3}$$
$$128 = 2s^2$$
Divide both sides by 2:
$$s^2 = 64$$
Take the positive square root:
$$s = 8$$
That matches Choice A — $8$.
